name: @forwardimpact/gear
description: Shared libraries and services for platform builders and agents — CLIs, retrieval, evaluation, and infrastructure published to npm.
author: D. Olsson <hi@senzilla.io>

jobs.trigger: Building an agent that needs structured knowledge, typed service contracts, or self-documenting CLIs — and the alternative is reimplementing retrieval, transport, and interface plumbing from scratch.
jobs.bigHire: give humans and agents shared capabilities through the same interface, with tooling to prove changes actually improved outcomes.
jobs.littleHire: query RDF graphs, run vector search, or expose a gRPC service as MCP tools without building retrieval or transport from scratch.
jobs.competesWith: hand-rolled retrieval; bespoke gRPC wrappers; ad-hoc CLI frameworks; monolithic agent platforms that bundle everything

forces.push: Every new agent project rebuilds the same retrieval, transport, and interface plumbing.
forces.pull: Focused, interoperable libraries that work standalone or together — humans and agents on the same docs.
forces.habit: Copy-pasting infrastructure from the last project rather than using shared, tested primitives.
forces.anxiety: Depending on shared libraries means losing control over the interface contract.

jobs.firedWhen: the libraries diverge from how agents actually consume them; or a monolithic framework absorbs enough of the surface that individual libraries feel like overhead.

jobs.trigger: An agent change shipped but nobody can tell whether it actually improved outcomes — the only evidence is anecdotal.
jobs.bigHire: prove whether agent changes improved outcomes with reproducible evidence.
jobs.littleHire: generate deterministic test data from a DSL and chart agent metrics with XmR control charts to distinguish signal from noise.
jobs.competesWith: manual spot-checking; anecdotal feedback; shipping without measuring; building custom eval harnesses per project

forces.push: Agent improvements are claimed but never measured — regressions hide until users complain.
forces.pull: Reproducible eval datasets and control charts that make real improvement visible.
forces.habit: Shipping agent changes and assuming they helped because the prompt looks better.
forces.anxiety: Rigorous evaluation might reveal that recent changes made things worse.

jobs.firedWhen: eval overhead exceeds the value of knowing; or the team stops iterating on agents.
